subject: Geography
title: Land, Soil, Water, Natural Vegetation and Wildlife Resources

questions.text: What percentage of the Earth's surface is covered by land?
questions.explanation: Land covers only about 30% of the Earth's surface and not all of it is habitable.

questions.text: The uneven distribution of population in different parts of the world is mainly due to varied characteristics of ________.
questions.explanation: Rugged topography and extreme climates are sparsely populated compared to plains.

questions.text: Land used for common purposes like collection of fodder, fruits, or medicinal herbs is called:
questions.explanation: Community lands are also called common property resources.

questions.text: The thin layer of grainy substance covering the surface of the earth is called:
questions.explanation: Soil is closely linked to the land and formed by weathered rocks.

questions.text: What determines the fertility of soil?
options: Amount of organic matter and minerals
questions.explanation: The right mix of minerals and organic matter (humus) makes the soil fertile.

questions.text: The process of breaking up and decay of exposed rocks is called:
questions.explanation: Weathering occurs due to temperature changes, frost action, plants, and animals.

questions.text: Which of the following is NOT a factor of soil formation?
questions.explanation: Texture is a characteristic of soil, not a factor in its formation.

questions.text: How many years does it take to make just one centimeter of soil?
questions.explanation: Soil formation is a very slow process.

questions.text: Which method of soil conservation involves building barriers of stones, grass or soil along contours?
questions.explanation: Contour barriers help prevent water from washing away the soil.

questions.text: What is the method of soil conservation in which bare ground between plants is covered with a layer of organic matter like straw?
questions.explanation: Mulching helps retain soil moisture.

questions.text: Which method of soil conservation is most effective in coastal and dry regions?
questions.explanation: Rows of trees are planted to check the wind movement to protect soil cover.

questions.text: About ________ of the Earth's surface is covered with water.
questions.explanation: Earth is often called the 'Water Planet' because of its vast oceans.

questions.text: Fresh water accounts for only about ________ of the total water on Earth.
questions.explanation: Most of the water on Earth is saline (salty).

questions.text: The process by which water continually changes its form and circulates between oceans, atmosphere and land is:
questions.explanation: Water is neither added nor subtracted from Earth; it just changes form.

questions.text: Which of the following is a major cause of water pollution?
questions.explanation: Industrial effluents and sewage contaminate water bodies with nitrates and pesticides.

questions.text: The narrow zone of contact between the lithosphere, hydrosphere and atmosphere is called:
questions.explanation: Life exists only in the biosphere.

questions.text: In the biosphere, living beings are inter-related and interdependent on each other for survival. This life-supporting system is called:
questions.explanation: The ecosystem is the functional unit where living things interact.

questions.text: Vultures in the Indian subcontinent were dying of kidney failure due to a drug called ________ used to treat livestock.
questions.explanation: This drug is a painkiller that was found to be toxic to vultures when they scavenged carcasses.

questions.text: The growth of vegetation depends primarily on ________.
questions.explanation: Vegetation types like forests and grasslands are determined by climatic conditions.

questions.text: Short stunted trees and grasses grow in the regions of ________.
questions.explanation: Moderate rainfall leads to the formation of grasslands.

questions.text: Thorny shrubs and scrubs grow in ________ regions.
questions.explanation: Plants in dry areas have deep roots and waxy leaves to reduce transpiration.

questions.text: Forests in which trees shed their leaves in a particular season are called:
questions.explanation: This is done to conserve moisture.

questions.text: Which international convention lists species of animals and birds in which trade is prohibited?
questions.explanation: CITES stands for Convention on International Trade in Endangered Species of Wild Fauna and Flora.

questions.text: What is the primary factor responsible for landslides in hilly areas?
questions.explanation: Landslides are mass movements of rock or debris down a slope triggered by seismic or weather events.

questions.text: Land reclamation, regulated use of chemical pesticides and fertilizers are methods of ________.
questions.explanation: These steps prevent land degradation.

questions.text: Humus is found in which layer of the soil?
questions.explanation: Top soil contains organic matter (humus) and fine particles.

questions.text: The availability of water per person is declining because of ________.
questions.explanation: Water scarcity is driven by high demand and pollution.

questions.text: Rainwater harvesting is a method to:
questions.explanation: It involves collecting and storing rainwater for future use.

questions.text: In the ________ system, water is saved by allowing it to reach the roots of plants drop by drop.
questions.explanation: Drip irrigation is highly efficient in dry regions.

questions.text: Mosses and lichens are part of ________ vegetation.
questions.explanation: Tundra vegetation is found in cold, polar climates.

questions.text: What is the full form of CITES?
options: Convention on International Trade in Endangered Species
Council for International Timber and Environmental Safety
Center for Indian Textile and Ethnic Styles
Committee for Internal Trade of Electronic Systems
questions.explanation: It is an agreement between governments to ensure trade doesn't threaten survival of wild species.

questions.text: Which of these is NOT a natural way of water conservation?
questions.explanation: Desalination is an expensive industrial process to remove salt from seawater.

questions.text: Parent rock determines the ________ of the soil.
questions.explanation: The original rock decides the mineral composition and appearance of the soil.

questions.text: Which factor determines the thickness of soil profile?
questions.explanation: Soil takes hundreds of years to form; thus time determines its depth.

questions.text: Land used for growing crops is called ________ land.
questions.explanation: Arable land is suitable for cultivation.

questions.text: Soil erosion is caused by ________.
questions.explanation: Both human and natural factors lead to soil degradation.

questions.text: Oceans are the major source of ________ in the atmosphere through the water cycle.
questions.explanation: Evaporation from oceans provides water vapor for clouds.

questions.text: The 'Shelter Belts' method of soil conservation is most common in ________.
questions.explanation: Trees are used as windbreaks to prevent wind erosion.

questions.text: Which layer of the Earth provides minerals for soil formation?
questions.explanation: The lithosphere is the rocky crust of the earth.

questions.text: Water is a ________ resource.
questions.explanation: Water circulates through the water cycle making it renewable, though fresh water is limited.
